original_reasoning: العتريف من الجمال=الشديد (strong, robust camel). πολύτροφος=well-fed, plump. A strong robust animal is often well-nourished. The causal link (good feeding produces strong animals) makes this a solid domain connection.
Arabic gloss is wool; Greek is lead goat. Both sit in the goat-fleece pastoral domain, so the conceptual field is plausible.
Arabic bulk and swelling can map to the idea of a high massive peak, so there is a plausible landform bridge.
Arabic benefit/usefulness can plausibly meet Greek you have taken/received through acquiring benefit.
Arabic qaṭṭ marks time-depth in the past, and Greek points to such great age; the time axis is shared.

Arabic khazm = nose-ring, hair-circle through nose-cartilage (the piercing/perforating action). Greek aichmazo = throw/hurl a spear (from aichme 'spear-point'). Both center on the sharp piercing implement/action. Strong concrete link.
Arabic focuses on gathering cud into one cheek, a clearly lateral or sideward placement, which plausibly matches Greek slanting or crosswise.
Arabic rolling downward can plausibly drift into rubbing away, wearing down, or consuming through repeated motion.
